shortAnswer: Triglycerides are blood fats that serve as an energy reserve. Elevated levels can increase the risk of cardiovascular disease and pancreatitis. Alcohol, sugar, and obesity play a major role. Discuss elevated values with your doctor.
metaDescription: Triglycerides elevated? Normal ranges, causes, and what you can do about high blood fats: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What dietary or lifestyle changes will lower my triglycerides?
Is there a risk of pancreatitis?
